title: abutilon affine
intro: Abutilon affine, also known as the flowering maple or parlor maple, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family. The leaves and flowers of Abutilon affine have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani systems, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, as well as supporting the immune system and digestive health. Abutilon affine is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and flowers are also used in herbal teas, tinctures, and ointments to leverage its medicinal properties.
